rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU ET AL VS. TINA YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 7, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED, in part and DENIED in part. Plaintiffs are not to change the case name on the caption page. First Amended Complaint to be filed and served forthwith. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT I BASSIN VS. BRENDAN LOFTUS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 7, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS Hearing Required. Parties to appear. Plaintiff to provide explanation as to why he would not have received Defendant's September 30, 2021 email regarding Defendant's application for rental assistance before Plaintiff filed his UD cover sheet on October 5, 2021. The email was sent to an address that was used by Plaintiff in 2021, as Plaintiff includes other emails to that address as exhibits. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARIE WHITIS VS. JESUS RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION TO PREVENT FORFEITURE DUE TO COVID-19 RENTAL DEBT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 7, 2022 line 8. DEFENDANT JESUS RODRIGUEZ'S APPLICATION TO PREVENT FORFEITURE DUE TO COVID-19 RENTAL DEBT Hearing Required. No later than April 7, 2022 by 9:00 a.m. Defendant to present evidence required by CCP 1179.13(a)(2)(B)(i): A copy of a final decision from the government rental assistance program showing the property address, the amount of payment approved, and the time period for which assistance was provided. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
